rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF SOFIA Y. NUNEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed since the last hearing. The judicial officer is to review the file and may discuss with counsel and determine whether to: 1) Discharge the minor's counsel at this time, given that counsel may have ethical duties not to take or propose any action without discussing first with the minor as counsel's "client" to ascertain her wishes (which counsel is unable to do, as the minor and her mother cannot be reached). 2) Allow the minor's counsel to petition for her fees. 3) Informally invite the public guardian again (or formally issue an order to appear at this time) to discuss: i) The public guardian's appointment as temporary guardian, either on the public guardian's petition for voluntary appointment under Probate Code section 2920(a)(2) or on the Court's own motion under section 2920(b) to give 15-day notice to the public guardian to apply for involuntary appointment. ii) Termination of the guardianship estate, on the public guardian's section 3411 petition as temporary guardian, and deposit of the remaining cash with the county treasurer under section 3412(e) to allow immediate discharge of the public guardian, unless the public guardian decides to continue serving as general guardian until the minor attains majority. 4) Issue an order to show cause to the guardian and her counsel and surety: i) Why the guardian should not be suspended in favor of the public guardian as temporary guardian. ii) Why the guardian should not be surcharged for unexplained disbursements. iii) Why the guardian should not be surcharged an additional amount representing the guardian's counsel's fees for multiple court appearances to explain the guardian's failure to file the subject accounting, if the estate is charged (which happens if the Court approves the fees in full on this petition). iv) Why a surcharge against the guardian, if unpaid, should not be considered a breach of condition of the guardian's bond (see section 2620.2(c)(3)). 5) Continue the accounting petition including the guardian's counsel's fee request until the public guardian is appointed as temporary guardian and takes possession of the estate cash, or take under submission to: i) Merely acknowledge (but not approve or even allow or settle) the account without giving res judicata effect otherwise avaiable under section 7250(a). ii) Reserve the surcharge issue for later determination. iii) Deny the request to reduce the guardian's bond until the surcharge issue is resolved. iv) Fix the guardian's counsel's fees and costs. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANK SHIGERU MATSUNO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/25. Examiner's Notes sent to attorney with the following issues: 1) Petitioner states that she has given instructions to liquidate various estate assets. A supplemental declaration will need to be filed once those assets are liquidated and funds deposited into the estate account, and a more current assets on hand list will need to be provided (specifying the as of date), along with a revised proposed distribution. The fee base will also need to be revised, if needed, based upon any loss on sales. The proposed distribution will be reviewed once this issue is addressed. 2) It does not appear that Attachment 1 of the I&A filed on 3/7/25 is accurate (e.g. petitioner alleges in paragraph 18, page 11 that the Rolls-Royce North American 401(k) will be liquidated and that $10,261.55 will be deposited into the estate checking account; however, that asset was listed as $31,689.23 in the I&A). The I&A must list the amounts payable upon death in lump amounts to the estate. 3) Petitioner requests direct distribution to the heirs of Mark Matsuno; however, he postdeceased, and no authority has been provided to allow for direct distribution. 4) There is over $877K in one bank. Evidence of full FDIC insurance coverage must be provided prior to this estate closing. 5) Petitioner requests $24,425.13 in reimbursements, but no substantiation has been provided. 6) The following have not been filed: a. Notice of Hearing b. Waivers of accounting c. Proposed order

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL J. TEDESCHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Exhibit C to the petition shows 107.322 shares of First Eagle Global Fd having a "Fiduciary Acquisition Value" of $2,094.71, but 212.053 shares were inventoried and appraised at $9,790.49. (2) The 9,554.78 units of SIT New Beginning United St were appraised at $107,969.01 but are now shown with a "Fiduciary Acquisition Value" of just $10,789.01. (3) Several (at least nine) other securities (e.g., Payden Limited, Blackrock Strategic Income, Columbia Dividend Income, etc.) have a different "Fiduciary Acquisition Value" than their I&A appraisal. (4) Executor alleges not to have taken any action under her IAEA authority, but there are also additional securities on hand that were not inventoried. (5) Notice was mailed to Madelane Dixon at an address that differs from the one shown to be hers in the petition. (6) Notice was mailed to "Reagan" Robertson, but one of the waivers of accounting was signed by Raegan Robertson. (7) The statutory compensation calculation was also slightly off. (The executor was granted IAEA authority on 9/10/24, in the order appointing her, but she did not claim the authority by obtaining Letters until 10/15/24.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/17/25. It appears that an amendment to the petition should be filed to explain the actions the executor took and/or if the slightly higher statutory compensation will be requested. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOWARD LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption shows both Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration. It cannot be for both. (2) The Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete: no telephone number and not dated. (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) Item 8 fails to disclose if Mabel Leung was survived by any issue. If she was, they must be identified in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, and must receive notice. If she was not, the petitioner's verified supplement must be filed, setting this forth for the record. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/25/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] If the petitioner is, indeed, the sole heir, no notice is required, but if the decedent was survived any issue of his predeceased sister, notice to them of the 7/9/25 hearing must include these instructions attached to it.)
